title: Boesenbergia tillandsiodes
intro: Boesenbergia tillandsioides, commonly known as fingerroot, is a type of flowering plant in the family Zingiberaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in reducing pain and inflammation, as well as treating infections. Bioactive constituents of Boesenbergia tillandsioides include alkaloids, glycosides, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fingerroot is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are utilized in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Boesenbergia tillandsioides is classified within the family Zingiberaceae. This family consists of plants commonly known as gingers.
